title: Acceptance Criteria (AC) emission
when_to_read: Before writing any test during a `build`-phase task. The mechanism is non-obvious and easy to skip; an untagged test is invisible to Memex.
body:
> **No official helper for your stack?** If you can't `npm install @memex-ai-ac/vitest` (you're in Python, Go, Ruby, etc.), call `get_information(topic='ac-emission-bootstrap')` for a language-agnostic spec to hand-roll a correct emitter. The rest of this topic assumes the official helper.

## The mental model: the ref IS the routing instruction

**The AC ref's namespace IS the routing instruction.** You do not set an env var. The helper reads the namespace prefix of every `tagAc(...)` call and routes the emission to that namespace's canonical Memex server:

- `mindset-int/...`  → `https://int.memex.ai`
- `mindset-prod/...` → `https://memex.ai`

This default routing IS the safety mechanism. Emitting to localhost when the ref names `mindset-prod` is a category error — events would land in the wrong database, the spec on prod would never see them, and the agent would falsely report tests as run.

If the ref names a namespace the helper doesn't recognise (a customer tenant, a new env) AND no override is set, the helper defaults to the SaaS host `https://memex.ai` (which serves every customer namespace) rather than skipping. It never falls through to localhost. `mindset-int` keeps its explicit mapping; self-hosted or local setups set `MEMEX_TEST_EVENTS_URL`.

## AC kinds

An AC is a forward-facing testable assertion about what the system must do, addressable as `ac-N` per Spec. Two flavours:

- **Scope ACs** — manager-authored, plain-English outcome commitments (the product audience). Typically created with the Spec at draft/early-specify via `create_ac({kind:'scope', ...})`. Direct parent is the Spec itself.
- **Implementation ACs** — agent-spawned from resolved Decisions; technical, mechanism-shaped; auto-accepted as `active`. Created via `create_ac({kind:'implementation', parent_decision_ref:'.../decisions/dec-N', ...})`. Direct parent is one or more Decisions.

`list_acs({ref})` lists ACs on a Spec, filterable by `kind` and `status`. `get_ac({ref})` returns one AC.

## When implementing an Implementation AC (during `build`)

You are likely working in `build` with a list of Implementation ACs to deliver. For each AC:

1. **Confirm the emission helper is installed.** As of v0.1.0 the canonical install is via npm:

   ```bash
   npm install --save-dev @memex-ai-ac/vitest
   ```

   Then wire it into your `vitest.config.ts`:

   ```typescript
   import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config';

   export default defineConfig({
     test: {
       setupFiles: ['@memex-ai-ac/vitest/setup'],
     },
   });
   ```

   For frameworks other than Vitest (Jest, pytest, etc.), check `<server>/docs/examples/` for reference implementations. If your framework isn't documented yet, port the wire format (see below) and the namespace-routing rules to your framework.

2. **Write the production code** that makes the AC true.
3. **Write one or more tests** that assert the AC empirically (unit, integration, AST, schema introspection, snapshot — whatever shape fits the assertion).
4. **Tag each test with the AC** via `tagAc('<canonical-ac-ref>')` imported from `@memex-ai-ac/vitest`. The helper handles the per-test emission automatically: on every test run, pass or fail, it POSTs a `(ac_uid, status, test_identifier, duration_ms)` event to the workspace.

Without the emission, the workspace cannot track whether the AC is verified — a silent passing test is, to Memex, a test that doesn't exist.

## Authentication — the emission key (REQUIRED)

`/api/test-events` requires a valid per-Memex **emission key**. Without it, every emission is rejected `401 Unauthorized` and the AC is never recorded.

- Set it as an environment variable wherever tests run: `MEMEX_EMIT_KEY=mxk_… npm test`. The helper attaches it as `Authorization: Bearer <key>` on every POST.
- In CI, store it as a secret named `MEMEX_EMIT_KEY` (e.g. a GitHub Actions / GitLab CI variable); the helper picks it up automatically.
- A key authorises emissions ONLY for the Memex named in your `ac_uid`s (the `<namespace>/<memex>` prefix). A key from a different Memex is rejected `401`.
- A missing or invalid key never FAILS the test run — the helper logs a warning and moves on — it just means emissions silently don't land. So a green suite with a spec stuck at 0% verified usually means `MEMEX_EMIT_KEY` isn't set.

### You cannot mint a key — ask the human

**The coding agent must NOT and CANNOT mint emission keys.** Keys are generated only by a human in the Memex UI (Settings → Emission Keys), where the raw key is shown exactly once. This is deliberate: a long-lived secret must never pass through an agent's context window. So when you hit a `401` from `/api/test-events` (or a spec sits at 0% verified because emissions are rejected), STOP and ask the human — for example:

> "Emissions to <memex> are unauthorized. Please generate an emission key in Memex settings (Settings → Emission Keys) and set it as MEMEX_EMIT_KEY in this project's environment / CI secrets."

Do not work around a 401 by disabling emission, redirecting the destination, or fabricating a key.

## Three adopter controls (v0.1.0)

The helper exposes three independent controls. All default to no-ops so doing nothing matches today's behaviour.

### `MEMEX_EMIT` — gate the emission

When you don't want emissions from a particular environment (typically developer laptops), set the env var:

```bash
MEMEX_EMIT=false npm test
```

The helper makes zero HTTP requests. Accepted off-values (case-insensitive): `false`, `0`, `no`, `off`. Default and any other value is `true`.

### `hidden: true` — opt one emission out of the dashboard

When you want the emission recorded for audit but not surfaced in the verification badge (typical case: iterating on a `done`-phase regression fix):

Per-call:

```typescript
tagAc('mindset-prod/your-memex/specs/spec-3/acs/ac-1', { hidden: true });
```

Or globally for one test run:

```bash
MEMEX_HIDDEN=true npm test
```

Hidden emissions are stored server-side (audit trail intact) but the AC's verification badge stays at the latest non-hidden emission.

### `metadata` — extensible context

The helper auto-populates well-known metadata keys when running in CI on GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, BuildKite, or CircleCI:

- `branch`, `commit`, `host`, `run_id`, `run_url`

(Note: `actor` is a top-level wire-format field — sibling of `hidden` and `metadata` — not a metadata key. See the wire-format section below.)

You can add your own keys per emission:

```typescript
tagAc('mindset-prod/your-memex/specs/spec-3/acs/ac-1', {
  metadata: { tenant: 'acme', feature_flag: 'rag_v2' },
});
```

Or globally via env vars:

```bash
MEMEX_METADATA_tenant=acme npm test
```

Server-side size limits: 4KB total, 32 keys, 256 chars per value. The server validates; oversized keys are dropped, the verification signal still lands, and an `X-Memex-Warning` response header names what was dropped.

**Important**: metadata is visible to anyone who can read the Memex, including anonymous visitors on public Memexes. Do not put sensitive values here.

## The wire format

The helper POSTs to `<canonical-host>/api/test-events` where the host is derived from the canonical ref's namespace per the routing table above (unknown namespaces default to `https://memex.ai`). The destination server validates that your emission key authorises the memex named in the ref; an event for a memex your key does not cover is rejected `401`.

Payload (JSON):

```json
{
  "ac_uid": "mindset-prod/memex-app/specs/spec-2/acs/ac-1",
  "status": "pass",
  "test_identifier": "tests/cache_test.py::test_uses_redis",
  "duration_ms": 42,
  "actor": "wic",
  "hidden": false,
  "metadata": { "branch": "main", "commit": "abc123" }
}
```

- `ac_uid` — the AC's full canonical ref (`<namespace>/<memex>/specs/spec-N/acs/ac-M`). The same string you get back from `list_acs` or `create_ac`. Don't use the bare `ac-N` handle alone (ambiguous across Specs).
- `status` — one of `pass`, `fail`, `error`.
- `test_identifier` — typically file path + function name; lets the workspace group events per test for flakiness analysis.
- `duration_ms`, `commit_sha`, `run_id` — optional.
- `actor` — optional string (v0.1.0, spec-115 dec-6). WHO ran the test. Top-level sibling of `hidden` and `metadata` because it's part of the cross-table activity contract (spec-122), not free-form provenance. Helper auto-populates from env vars (`GITHUB_ACTOR` → `GITLAB_USER_LOGIN` → `BUILDKITE_BUILD_AUTHOR` → `CIRCLE_USERNAME` → `USER` → `USERNAME`). A `metadata.actor` key is accepted opaquely but NOT promoted into this field; the top-level value is canonical.
- `hidden` — optional boolean (v0.1.0). When true, the event is stored but excluded from the AC's verification badge calculation.
- `metadata` — optional `Record<string, string>` (v0.1.0). Surfaced in the AC matrix tooltip in the UI. Server-side caps: 4KB total, 32 keys, 256-char values; over-cap keys are dropped, named in the `X-Memex-Warning` response header.

The helper must emit on EVERY test invocation — pass and fail alike — so the workspace sees the latest state.

## The override (almost never needed)

`MEMEX_TEST_EVENTS_URL=<full-url>` overrides the namespace routing. **Use this almost never** — typically only when you're explicitly testing the emission code path itself against a non-canonical host. For 99% of real test work, you do NOT set this. The namespace-derived routing is the right destination.

When the override is set AND the ref's namespace is in the helper's mapping table AND the override URL points somewhere different from the namespace's canonical base, the helper emits a loud one-time stderr warning per (namespace, override-URL) tuple so the override-defeats-safety situation is visible.

### ❌ Anti-example — what NOT to do

When reviewing the helper code or this topic, an agent who carries the standard "don't accidentally write to prod" defensive prior may reach for the override and point it at `http://localhost:8080/...` as the "conservative" choice. **This is a category error.**

> ❌ **"I'll set `MEMEX_TEST_EVENTS_URL=http://localhost:8080/api/test-events` to be safe before running these tests."**
>
> → Events for `mindset-prod/...` refs would land in your local Postgres (or nowhere), NOT the prod namespace's canonical Postgres. The default routing IS the safety; the override defeats it. The spec on prod sits at 0% verified while you report the tests as run.
>
> The right move is **to do nothing** — let the namespace-derivation logic route the event to the canonical destination. The destination server then validates your emission key against the memex named in the ref and accepts or rejects accordingly.

This exact mistake happened during the b-68 implementation thread and is the origin of the b-90 spec. The conflict warn, the destination-side emission-key check, and this anti-example are all in place so the next agent (and the agent after that) don't repeat it.

If you cannot determine the AC ref from your instructions, call `list_acs` on the Spec to find it. If no Implementation AC exists for the work you've been asked to do, surface that as a `question` comment to the user before writing speculative code.
